teaching_card.title: Circles (Medium)
teaching_card.body: For a circle of radius r: the circumference (distance around) is 2πr, and the area is πr². The diameter is twice the radius. Answers are often left 'in terms of π.'

screens.title: Radius, diameter, and π
screens.body: The radius r reaches from the center to the edge; the diameter is 2r. Most circle formulas are written 'in terms of π' — leave π in the answer rather than approximating.
key_points: Answers are often left in terms of π.

screens.title: Circumference and area
screens.body: Circumference (the distance around) = 2πr. Area (the space inside) = πr². The key difference: circumference uses r, area uses r². Mixing these is the most common mistake.

screens.title: Arcs and sectors
screens.body: An arc or a sector is a fraction of the whole circle — the fraction is (central angle)/360. Arc length = (θ/360)·2πr; sector area = (θ/360)·πr². Take the fraction of the full circumference or area.
key_points: Arc = fraction × 2πr; sector area = fraction × πr².

screens.title: Try the moves
screens.body: Radius 6: circumference 2π·6 = 12π, area π·6² = 36π. A 90° sector is ¼ of the circle, so its area is ¼·36π = 9π.
